introCards.title: What is Arbain Nawawi?
introCards.body: Arbain Nawawi is a collection of foundational hadith compiled by Imam al-Nawawi. The hadith cover major principles of Islam, iman, worship, manners, sincerity, halal and haram, and dealings with people. It is one of the most studied beginner texts in Islam.

introCards.title: Why Imam al-Nawawi Compiled It
introCards.body: Imam al-Nawawi selected hadith that represent major foundations of the religion. Many scholars considered some of these narrations to be among the central hadith of Islam. The aim is not only memorisation, but understanding the meanings and acting upon them.

introCards.title: Biography of Imam al-Nawawi
introCards.body: His full name was Yahya ibn Sharaf al-Nawawi. He was born in Nawa near Damascus in 631H and died in 676H. He pursued knowledge from a young age and became a major Shafi'i scholar and hadith scholar. He was known for discipline, worship, and service to the Ummah through works such as Riyadh al-Salihin, al-Adhkar, Sharh Sahih Muslim, and al-Arba'in al-Nawawiyyah.

introCards.title: How to Study Arbain Nawawi
introCards.body: Read the hadith carefully, understand the simple meaning, memorise gradually, and review regularly. Use the Daily Quiz to strengthen recall, then try to act upon what you learn. The goal is steady learning that increases love for the Sunnah and careful practice.

hadiths.title: Hadith 1: Intentions
hadiths.arabic: إِنَّمَا الْأَعْمَالُ بِالنِّيَّاتِ، وَإِنَّمَا لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مَا نَوَى، فَمَنْ كَانَتْ هِجْرَتُهُ إِلَى اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ فَهِجْرَتُهُ إِلَى اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ، وَمَنْ كَانَتْ هِجْرَتُهُ لِدُنْيَا يُصِيبُهَا أَوِ امْرَأَةٍ يَنْكِحُهَا فَهِجْرَتُهُ إِلَى مَا هَاجَرَ إِلَيْهِ.
hadiths.english: Actions are judged by intentions, and each person receives according to what they intended. A migration done for Allah and His Messenger is for Allah and His Messenger, while a migration done for a worldly aim is only for what the person intended.

vocabulary.meaning: Intention; the purpose held in the heart.
Migration; here used as an example of a major action.

lessons: Allah knows the inner purpose behind every action.
The same outward deed can have different value depending on intention.
A learner should regularly renew intention before worship and study.
chunks: فَمَنْ كَانَتْ هِجْرَتُهُ إِلَى اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ

questions.explanation: This hadith is narrated by Umar ibn al-Khattab.

questions.prompt: What is the main lesson of this hadith?
questions.explanation: The central lesson is that intention gives moral direction to actions.

questions.prompt: What comes next after: إِنَّمَا الْأَعْمَالُ
questions.explanation: The phrase is: إِنَّمَا الْأَعْمَالُ بِالنِّيَّاتِ.

hadiths.reflection: Before studying or doing worship, pause and ask: am I doing this to seek Allah's pleasure? This hadith teaches beginners that inner sincerity is not a side issue. It is part of how a deed is understood and valued.

hadiths.title: Hadith 2: Islam, Iman, Ihsan
hadiths.arabic: بَيْنَمَا نَحْنُ جُلُوسٌ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ، إِذْ طَلَعَ عَلَيْنَا رَجُلٌ شَدِيدُ بَيَاضِ الثِّيَابِ، شَدِيدُ سَوَادِ الشَّعَرِ، لَا يُرَى عَلَيْهِ أَثَرُ السَّفَرِ، وَلَا يَعْرِفُهُ مِنَّا أَحَدٌ، حَتَّى جَلَسَ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، فَأَسْنَدَ رُكْبَتَيْهِ إِلَى رُكْبَتَيْهِ، وَوَضَعَ كَفَّيْهِ عَلَى فَخِذَيْهِ، وَقَالَ: يَا مُحَمَّدُ، أَخْبِرْنِي عَنِ الْإِسْلَامِ. فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ: الْإِسْلَامُ أَنْ تَشْهَدَ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللهِ، وَتُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةَ، وَتُؤْتِيَ الزَّكَاةَ، وَتَصُومَ رَمَضَانَ، وَتَحُجَّ الْبَيْتَ إِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلًا. قَالَ: صَدَقْتَ. قَالَ: فَأَخْبِرْنِي عَنِ الْإِيمَانِ. قَالَ: أَنْ تُؤْمِنَ بِاللهِ، وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ، وَكُتُبِهِ، وَرُسُلِهِ، وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ، وَتُؤْمِنَ بِالْقَدَرِ خَيْرِهِ وَشَرِّهِ. قَالَ: صَدَقْتَ. قَالَ: فَأَخْبِرْنِي عَنِ الْإِحْسَانِ. قَالَ: أَنْ تَعْبُدَ اللهَ كَأَنَّكَ تَرَاهُ، فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُنْ تَرَاهُ فَإِنَّهُ يَرَاكَ. قَالَ: فَأَخْبِرْنِي عَنِ السَّاعَةِ. قَالَ: مَا الْمَسْؤُولُ عَنْهَا بِأَعْلَمَ مِنَ السَّائِلِ. قَالَ: فَأَخْبِرْنِي عَنْ أَمَارَاتِهَا. قَالَ: أَنْ تَلِدَ الْأَمَةُ رَبَّتَهَا، وَأَنْ تَرَى الْحُفَاةَ الْعُرَاةَ الْعَالَةَ رِعَاءَ الشَّاءِ يَتَطَاوَلُونَ فِي الْبُنْيَانِ. ثُمَّ انْطَلَقَ، فَلَبِثْتُ مَلِيًّا، ثُمَّ قَالَ: يَا عُمَرُ، أَتَدْرِي مَنِ السَّائِلُ؟ قُلْتُ: اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَعْلَمُ. قَالَ: فَإِنَّهُ جِبْرِيلُ أَتَاكُمْ يُعَلِّمُكُمْ دِينَكُمْ.
hadiths.english: Jibril came in the form of a man and asked the Prophet ﷺ about Islam, iman, ihsan, the Hour, and its signs. The Prophet ﷺ explained the outward pillars of Islam, the inner beliefs of iman, and ihsan: worshipping Allah with awareness that He sees you.

vocabulary.meaning: Submission to Allah, shown through the pillars of worship.
Faith and belief in Allah and what He revealed.
Excellence in worship with awareness of Allah.

lessons: The religion includes outward actions, inward belief, and spiritual excellence.
Asking clear questions is a powerful way to teach and learn.
A Muslim should worship with the awareness that Allah sees them.
chunks: الْإِسْلَامُ أَنْ تَشْهَدَ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ

questions.prompt: Who came to teach the companions in this hadith?
questions.explanation: At the end, the Prophet ﷺ explained that the questioner was Jibril.

questions.prompt: Which term means excellence in worship?
questions.explanation: Ihsan is to worship Allah as though you see Him, knowing that He sees you.

questions.prompt: What comes next after: أَنْ تَعْبُدَ اللهَ كَأَنَّكَ
questions.explanation: The phrase is: أَنْ تَعْبُدَ اللهَ كَأَنَّكَ تَرَاهُ.

hadiths.reflection: This hadith helps beginners see Islam as complete and balanced: actions, beliefs, and inner awareness. It also shows that learning religion can begin with simple, sincere questions asked in the right way.

hadiths.title: Hadith 3: The Pillars of Islam
hadiths.arabic: بُنِيَ الْإِسْلَامُ عَلَى خَمْسٍ: شَهَادَةِ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللهِ، وَإِقَامِ الصَّلَاةِ، وَإِيتَاءِ الزَّكَاةِ، وَحَجِّ الْبَيْتِ، وَصَوْمِ رَمَضَانَ.
hadiths.english: Islam is built upon five foundations: testifying that none has the right to be worshipped except Allah and that Muhammad is His Messenger, establishing prayer, giving zakat, performing Hajj, and fasting Ramadan.

vocabulary.meaning: The required charity from eligible wealth.

lessons: The five pillars are foundations, not the whole building of Islam.
Prayer is described as something to establish, not merely to perform casually.
The pillars teach commitment through belief, worship, giving, fasting, and pilgrimage.
chunks: شَهَادَةِ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ

questions.prompt: How many pillars are mentioned in this hadith?
questions.explanation: The hadith says Islam is built upon five.

questions.prompt: Which pillar is described as establishing something?
questions.explanation: The hadith says إِقَامِ الصَّلَاةِ, establishing prayer.

questions.prompt: What comes next after: بُنِيَ الْإِسْلَامُ عَلَى
questions.explanation: The phrase is: بُنِيَ الْإِسْلَامُ عَلَى خَمْسٍ.

hadiths.reflection: This hadith gives a beginner a clear map of Islam's foundations. It should encourage steady learning: understand the testimony, guard the prayer, give what is due, fast Ramadan, and learn Hajj when able.

hadiths.title: Hadith 4: Human Creation and Final Deeds
hadiths.arabic: إِنَّ أَحَدَكُمْ يُجْمَعُ خَلْقُهُ فِي بَطْنِ أُمِّهِ أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْمًا نُطْفَةً، ثُمَّ يَكُونُ عَلَقَةً مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ، ثُمَّ يَكُونُ مُضْغَةً مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ، ثُمَّ يُرْسَلُ إِلَيْهِ الْمَلَكُ فَيَنْفُخُ فِيهِ الرُّوحَ، وَيُؤْمَرُ بِأَرْبَعِ كَلِمَاتٍ: بِكَتْبِ رِزْقِهِ، وَأَجَلِهِ، وَعَمَلِهِ، وَشَقِيٌّ أَوْ سَعِيدٌ. فَوَاللهِ الَّذِي لَا إِلَهَ غَيْرُهُ، إِنَّ أَحَدَكُمْ لَيَعْمَلُ بِعَمَلِ أَهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ حَتَّى مَا يَكُونُ بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَهَا إِلَّا ذِرَاعٌ، فَيَسْبِقُ عَلَيْهِ الْكِتَابُ فَيَعْمَلُ بِعَمَلِ أَهْلِ النَّارِ فَيَدْخُلُهَا، وَإِنَّ أَحَدَكُمْ لَيَعْمَلُ بِعَمَلِ أَهْلِ النَّارِ حَتَّى مَا يَكُونُ بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَهَا إِلَّا ذِرَاعٌ، فَيَسْبِقُ عَلَيْهِ الْكِتَابُ فَيَعْمَلُ بِعَمَلِ أَهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ فَيَدْخُلُهَا.
hadiths.english: The Prophet ﷺ described stages of human creation in the womb, the angel's writing of provision, lifespan, deeds, and final outcome, and the importance of how a person's life ends.

vocabulary.meaning: Appointed lifespan or time of death.

lessons: Allah knows a person's beginning, provision, lifespan, deeds, and final outcome.
A believer should keep working and not become complacent about past deeds.
Final deeds matter, so a Muslim keeps asking Allah for a good ending.

questions.explanation: This hadith is narrated by Abdullah ibn Mas'ud.
The hadith encourages humility, effort, and asking Allah for a good ending.

questions.prompt: What comes next after: فِي بَطْنِ
questions.explanation: The phrase is: فِي بَطْنِ أُمِّهِ.

hadiths.reflection: This hadith should not make a beginner passive. The commentary tradition highlights that people do not know their decree, so they keep working, repenting, and seeking Allah's help until the end of life.

hadiths.title: Hadith 5: Following the Sunnah
hadiths.arabic: مَنْ أَحْدَثَ فِي أَمْرِنَا هَذَا مَا لَيْسَ مِنْهُ فَهُوَ رَدٌّ. وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ لِمُسْلِمٍ: مَنْ عَمِلَ عَمَلًا لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ أَمْرُنَا فَهُوَ رَدٌّ.
hadiths.english: Whoever introduces into this matter of ours what is not from it will have it rejected. In another wording: whoever does an action that is not according to our matter will have it rejected.

vocabulary.meaning: Our matter; here, the religion and guidance of Islam.

lessons: Sincerity alone is not enough; worship must also follow revealed guidance.
This hadith is a basic measure for outward religious practice.
A beginner should learn worship from sound sources and qualified teachers.
chunks: مَنْ عَمِلَ عَمَلًا لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ أَمْرُنَا

questions.explanation: This hadith is narrated by Aisha bint Abi Bakr.

questions.prompt: What does this hadith mainly protect?
options: Following the Prophet's guidance in religion
questions.explanation: It teaches that religious deeds must follow the guidance of the Prophet ﷺ.
The phrase is: فَهُوَ رَدٌّ, meaning it is rejected.

hadiths.reflection: This hadith balances Hadith 1. Intention matters, but worship also needs correct guidance. Beginners should see this as encouragement to learn the Sunnah carefully, not as a reason to argue without knowledge.
